LT:CharacterLiteral.Definition: A {c:bold}character{/c} literal represents a single character enclosed in single quotes {c:color-orange keyword}' '{/c}. It is commonly used to work with individual characters.

LT:QuotedString.Definition: {c:bold}Quoted{/c} strings represent a sequence of characters enclosed in double quotes {c:color-orange keyword}" "{/c} on a single line.

LT:VerbatimString.Definition: {c:bold}Verbatim{/c} strings represent text exactly as written, without processing escape sequences. It allows multi-line text and uses the {c:color-orange keyword}@{/c} prefix before the opening quote.

LT:InterpolatedString.Definition: {c:bold}Interpolated{/c} strings allow expressions to be embedded directly within text using placeholders enclosed in braces {c:keyword}{ }{/c}. They use the {c:color-orange keyword}${/c} prefix before the opening quote and automatically convert embedded values to strings.

LT:InterpolatedVerbatimString.Definition: {c:bold}Interpolated{/c} {c:bold}verbatim{/c} strings support interpolation and multi-line text. They use either the {c:color-orange keyword}$@{/c} or {c:color-orange keyword}@${/c} prefix before the opening quote and automatically convert embedded values to strings.

LT:DecimalLiteral.Definition: The {c:color-light-green keyword}m{/c} suffix on a numeric literal specifies the {c:color-blue keyword}decimal{/c} type.

LT:FloatingPointLiteral.Definition: The {c:color-light-green keyword}f{/c} suffix on a numeric literal specifies the {c:color-blue keyword}float{/c} type.

LT:NumericLiteral.Definition: {c:bold}Numeric literals{/c} represent integers written directly in the code.
